This foundational approach enables: * **Fault Isolation:** If one component fails, doesn’t bring down entire system. * **Independent Scaling:** You allocate resources high-demand components without scaling entire app. * **Parallel dev:** Different teams build, test, and deploy their parts system concurrently without stepping each other's toes. * **Maintainability & Flexibility:** vastly easier update, debug, swap out specific technology single module than refactor monolithic codebase. --- ### qwopus3.6-27b-v2 single most important principle system design **Trade-offs**. **Why?** Because no system perfect across all dimensions simultaneously. ## Judge Analysis (structural extraction) { "consensus": [ { "point": "Trade-offs foundational principle system design", "panelists": [ "gpt-5.5", "qwopus3.6-27b-v2" ] }, { "point": "Modularity / decomposition into smaller independent units essential", "panelists": [ "claude-opus-4-8", "glm-5.2" ] }, { "point": "Complexity core problem that design must address", "panelists": [ "claude-opus-4-8", "glm-5.2" ] }, { "point": "System failure tied poor design choices (unmanaged tradeoffs, hidden bugs, unisolated faults)", "panelists": [ "claude-opus-4-8", "gpt-5.5", "glm-5.2" ] } ], "contradictions": [ { "topic": single most important principle", "positions": [ { "panelist": "claude-opus-4-8", "claim": "Managing complexity root principle;
